problemTitle: Insert Greatest Common Divisors in Linked List

sources.code:
# Time:  O(n)
# Space: O(1)

# linked list
class Solution(object):
    def insertGreatestCommonDivisors(self, head):
        """
        :type head: Optional[ListNode]
        :rtype: Optional[ListNode]
        """
        def gcd(a, b):
            while b:
                a, b = b, a%b
            return a

        curr = head
        while curr.next:
            curr.next = ListNode(gcd(curr.val, curr.next.val), curr.next)
            curr = curr.next.next
        return head

# Time:  O(n)
# Space: O(1)

# linked list
class Solution(object):
    def insertGreatestCommonDivisors(self, head):
        """
        :type head: Optional[ListNode]
        :rtype: Optional[ListNode]
        """
        def gcd(a, b):
            while b:
                a, b = b, a%b
            return a

        curr = head
        while curr.next:
            curr.next = ListNode(gcd(curr.val, curr.next.val), curr.next)
            curr = curr.next.next
        return head
sources.reviewedCode:
# Time:  O(n)
# Space: O(1)

# linked list
# [Reader note] Entry point
# Start from the core routine and keep this mental model in view: Handle one original pair at a time and be explicit about where traversal resumes after insertion.
class Solution(object):
    def insertGreatestCommonDivisors(self, head):
        """
        # [Reader note] State setup
        # This setup stage creates the state needed for the invariant or partial result: Preserving access before insertion, pairwise processing, and advancing correctly after mutating the list.
        :type head: Optional[ListNode]
        :rtype: Optional[ListNode]
        """
        def gcd(a, b):
            # [Reader note] Main pass
            # This is the main sweep where the implementation earns its efficiency instead of restarting work from scratch.
            while b:
                a, b = b, a%b
            return a

        curr = head
        while curr.next:
            curr.next = ListNode(gcd(curr.val, curr.next.val), curr.next)
            curr = curr.next.next
        # [Reader note] Return
        # The final return exposes the result after the traversal and state updates have settled.
        return head

sources.localPath: doocs_leetcode/solution/2800-2899/2807.Insert Greatest Common Divisors in Linked List/Solution.py
sources.relativePath: solution/2800-2899/2807.Insert Greatest Common Divisors in Linked List/Solution.py
sources.code:
# Definition for singly-linked list.
# class ListNode:
#     def __init__(self, val=0, next=None):
#         self.val = val
#         self.next = next
class Solution:
    def insertGreatestCommonDivisors(
        self, head: Optional[ListNode]
    ) -> Optional[ListNode]:
        pre, cur = head, head.next
        while cur:
            x = gcd(pre.val, cur.val)
            pre.next = ListNode(x, cur)
            pre, cur = cur, cur.next
        return head
sources.originalCode:
# Definition for singly-linked list.
# class ListNode:
#     def __init__(self, val=0, next=None):
#         self.val = val
#         self.next = next
class Solution:
    def insertGreatestCommonDivisors(
        self, head: Optional[ListNode]
    ) -> Optional[ListNode]:
        pre, cur = head, head.next
        while cur:
            x = gcd(pre.val, cur.val)
            pre.next = ListNode(x, cur)
            pre, cur = cur, cur.next
        return head
sources.reviewedCode:
# Definition for singly-linked list.
# class ListNode:
# [Reader note] Entry point
# Start from the core routine and keep this mental model in view: Handle one original pair at a time and be explicit about where traversal resumes after insertion.
#     def __init__(self, val=0, next=None):
# [Reader note] State setup
# This setup stage creates the state needed for the invariant or partial result: Preserving access before insertion, pairwise processing, and advancing correctly after mutating the list.
#         self.val = val
#         self.next = next
class Solution:
    def insertGreatestCommonDivisors(
        self, head: Optional[ListNode]
    ) -> Optional[ListNode]:
        pre, cur = head, head.next
        # [Reader note] Main pass
        # This is the main sweep where the implementation earns its efficiency instead of restarting work from scratch.
        while cur:
            x = gcd(pre.val, cur.val)
            pre.next = ListNode(x, cur)
            pre, cur = cur, cur.next
        # [Reader note] Return
        # The final return exposes the result after the traversal and state updates have settled.
        return head
